source: Authoritative bulletin database -- migrated from hardcoded NETAPP_SECURITY_BULLETIN_DB in app.js. Grows via POST /api/bulletins (daily scan agent).

bulletins.title: ONTAP Snapshot Locking Authorization Bypass
bulletins.description: A privileged remote attacker can set the expiry time of a locked snapshot to 'none', bypassing WORM snapshot protections and enabling deletion of locked snapshots before their retention period expires. High operational impact in ransomware recovery scenarios.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to ONTAP 9.16.1P9 or 9.17.1P2. Restrict management interface access to trusted networks. Enable audit logging on snapshot operations.

bulletins.title: ONTAP S3 NAS Bucket Directory Listing Information Disclosure
bulletins.description: An authenticated attacker can view directory contents of S3 NAS buckets for which they do not have proper permissions on ONTAP 9.12.1 and later with S3 NAS buckets enabled.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to the applicable fixed patch release for your ONTAP branch. Restrict S3 bucket access policies.

bulletins.title: ONTAP FreeBSD Underlying OS Vulnerability — Data Modification Risk
bulletins.description: All supported versions of FreeBSD are susceptible to CVE-2024-45289 which ONTAP uses as its underlying OS; successful exploitation could allow addition or modification of data.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply applicable ONTAP patch releases. Monitor NTAP-20250110-0001 for specific fixed versions per branch.

bulletins.category: Remote Code Execution / Denial of Service
bulletins.title: ONTAP OpenSSH regreSSHion — Signal Handler Race Condition RCE
bulletins.description: A race condition in OpenSSH sshd signal handler (regreSSHion) could theoretically allow remote unauthenticated code execution; ONTAP default sshd settings reduce exploitability and no exploit demonstrated on FreeBSD.

ontap: Later P-releases per branch — check NTAP-20240701-0001

bulletins.mitigation: Apply relevant ONTAP patch. Restrict SSH management access to trusted management VLANs only. Verify LoginGraceTime and MaxStartups are at ONTAP defaults.

bulletins.title: ONTAP REST API Privilege Escalation — Multi-Account Role Confusion
bulletins.description: An authenticated user with multiple remote accounts having different roles can perform actions via the ONTAP REST API exceeding their intended privileges, potentially enabling limited config/metrics access, setting modifications, or DoS.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to the fixed patch version. Audit user accounts for multiple role assignments. Enforce least-privilege principle in RBAC.

bulletins.title: ONTAP OpenSSH Pre-Auth Denial of Service (SSH2_MSG_PING Flood)
bulletins.description: An unauthenticated attacker can flood ONTAP's OpenSSH daemon with SSH2_MSG_PING packets during pre-authentication, causing uncontrolled memory consumption and management SSH service unavailability.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to fixed ONTAP 9.16.1 patch. Restrict SSH management access to trusted IPs via LIF firewall/service policy. Rate-limit SSH connections at network level.

bulletins.category: Mixed — DoS / Information Disclosure / Remote Impact
bulletins.title: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities (ONTAP: CVE-2024-42516)
bulletins.description: Multiple CVEs in Apache HTTP Server prior to 2.4.64 affect various NetApp products. ONTAP 9 is specifically listed as affected by CVE-2024-42516. CVE-2025-23048 (CVSS 9.1, Critical) affects other NetApp products within this advisory.

ontap: ONTAP patches incorporating Apache HTTP Server 2.4.64+

bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch releases incorporating Apache HTTP Server 2.4.64 or later. Check NTAP-20250718-0013 for product-specific fix versions.

bulletins.category: Use-After-Free / Denial of Service / Information Disclosure
bulletins.title: ONTAP libxml2 Use-After-Free Vulnerability
bulletins.description: A use-after-free vulnerability in libxml2 triggered by a crafted XML document validated against a schema with identity constraints can lead to information disclosure, data modification, or DoS in ONTAP and other NetApp products.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch incorporating fixed libxml2. Restrict XML processing surfaces. Check NTAP-20250328-0010 for per-version fix details.

bulletins.title: ONTAP libxslt Vulnerability — Unauthorized Data Modification / DoS
bulletins.description: A vulnerability in libxslt versions prior to 1.1.43 could allow unauthorized addition or modification of data or Denial of Service in ONTAP and other NetApp products.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch incorporating libxslt 1.1.43+. Check NTAP-20250613-0006 for per-version patch availability.

bulletins.title: OpenSSL Vulnerability in ONTAP 9.18.1 Only
bulletins.description: Multiple NetApp products incorporating OpenSSL 3.4–3.6 are susceptible to a vulnerability permitting information disclosure, data modification, or DoS. Only ONTAP 9.18.1 is affected in the ONTAP product line.
bulletins.mitigation: Monitor NTAP-20260204-0012 for ONTAP 9.18.1 patch release. If on 9.18.1, assess risk and plan patch upgrade.

bulletins.title: Apache Netty HTTP/2 MadeYouReset DDoS in NetApp Products
bulletins.description: A logical flaw in Netty's HTTP/2 implementation allows malformed control frames to bypass maximum concurrent streams limits, enabling resource exhaustion and distributed DDoS in multiple NetApp products.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp product patches incorporating Netty 4.1.124.Final+. Limit exposure of management HTTP/2 endpoints.

bulletins.title: Multiple Golang Vulnerabilities in NetApp Products including Trident
bulletins.description: Multiple CVEs in bundled Golang components affect various NetApp products including Trident and Trident Protect. Advisory clarifies which CVEs are applicable per product version.

trident: Latest Trident release incorporating fixed Golang — check NTAP-20260424-0001

bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Trident to the latest supported release. False-positive scanner alerts for Trident container images are common — always verify against official NTAP advisory before treating as confirmed.

bulletins.title: Java Platform SE Hotspot Vulnerability in NetApp Products
bulletins.description: A difficult-to-exploit vulnerability in Oracle Java SE Hotspot allows an unauthenticated network attacker to gain unauthorized read/write access to data accessible by the Java component in affected NetApp products.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP and SnapCenter patches incorporating Java CPU January 2025 updates. Monitor NTAP-20250124-0009 for final status.

bulletins.title: SnapCenter Critical Privilege Escalation to Remote Admin
bulletins.description: An authenticated low-privileged SnapCenter Server user can escalate privileges to administrator on any remote system where a SnapCenter plug-in is installed, via improper access control over internal REST API calls. CVSS 9.9 Critical.
bulletins.mitigation: IMMEDIATELY upgrade SnapCenter to 6.0.1P1 or 6.1P1. Audit SnapCenter user accounts for unauthorized privilege changes. Review all plug-in host access logs.

bulletins.title: SnapCenter Plaintext Credential Disclosure
bulletins.description: An authenticated SnapCenter attacker can discover plaintext credentials stored or transmitted by SnapCenter versions prior to 5.0p1.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to SnapCenter 5.0p1 or later. Rotate any credentials that may have been exposed.

bulletins.title: SnapCenter Improper Authorization — Logging Configuration Modification
bulletins.description: An authenticated SnapCenter user can modify system logging configuration settings, potentially enabling sensitive information disclosure or unauthorized data modification.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to SnapCenter 5.0 or later.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID SSRF via SSO Microsoft Entra ID — Config Deletion / DoS
bulletins.description: An authenticated low-privileged attacker can exploit an SSRF flaw in the SSO/Microsoft Entra ID integration to delete configuration data or deny access to resources. Only affects deployments with SSO + Entra ID configured.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.9.0.12 or 12.0.0.4. If upgrade is delayed, disable SSO with Microsoft Entra ID as interim workaround.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID Unauthenticated DoS on Admin Node
bulletins.description: An unauthenticated remote attacker can cause a Denial of Service condition on the StorageGRID Admin node via asymmetric resource consumption (CWE-405).
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.8.0.15 or 11.9.0.8. Restrict Admin node access to trusted management networks.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID Privilege Escalation — Grid Node Discovery / Storage Grade Modification
bulletins.description: An authenticated low-privileged attacker can discover Grid node names and IP addresses, or modify Storage Grades, enabling unauthorized data exposure or data modification.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.8.0.15 or 11.9.0.8.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID Authenticated DoS — Service Crash
bulletins.description: An authenticated attacker can cause a service crash in StorageGRID versions prior to 11.9.0.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.9.0 or later.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID Out-of-Memory / Node Reboot DoS
bulletins.description: An authenticated attacker can trigger an out-of-memory condition or node reboot via a specially crafted request in StorageGRID versions prior to 11.8.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.8 or later.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID Reflected XSS — Configuration Modification
bulletins.description: A reflected XSS vulnerability requires an attacker to know specific instance information and trick a privileged user into clicking a crafted link, potentially enabling unauthorized configuration changes or user account manipulation.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.8 or later. Enforce browser security headers.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID Metrics Query Information Disclosure
bulletins.description: An authenticated attacker with low privileges can run arbitrary metrics queries to reveal sensitive metric data they are not authorized to see.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to StorageGRID 11.9.0.13 or 12.0.0.6 or later.

bulletins.title: NetApp SAN Host Utilities for Windows Installer Local Privilege Escalation
bulletins.description: The installer for SAN Host Utilities for Windows is susceptible to a local privilege escalation vulnerability. A local user could exploit this to modify data, disclose sensitive information, or cause DoS.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade SAN Host Utilities for Windows to version 8.0.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.16.1/9.17.1 Node Instability — Unexpected Reboots (SU611 / mgwd / secd)
bulletins.description: Memory exhaustion under heavy workload on AFF A20/A30/A50, ASA, FAS50, FAS2720, AFF A220 triggers EMS alert wafl.memory.statusVeryLowMemory, causing mgwd/secd/vldb/bcomd processes to become unresponsive (>210s), triggering watchdog panic and unexpected node reboot.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to 9.16.1P11, 9.17.1P4, or 9.18.1. Review EMS logs for wafl.memory.statusVeryLowMemory events. Open support case referencing CONTAP-539599 if reboots observed.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.16.1/9.17.1 Intel E810 NIC Memory Leak — Nodewatchdog Panic
bulletins.description: A memory leak in the Intel E810 network adapter driver causes gradual BSD memory exhaustion on ONTAP nodes with E810 NICs, eventually triggering nodewatchdog panics and unexpected node reboots.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to 9.16.1P11 or 9.17.1P4. Verify NIC model via 'system node hardware show'. Reference CONTAP-480665 in support case.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.17.1 System Manager 'API not found' Errors on ASAr1 Systems
bulletins.description: In ONTAP System Manager 9.17.1 GA on ASAr1 systems, multiple workflows fail with 'API not found' errors, affecting dashboard health cards, mediator visibility, node discovery, software update display, IPSpaces in network overview, and MetroCluster detection.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to ONTAP 9.17.1P2+. Use ONTAP CLI as workaround for affected System Manager workflows.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.17.1 Spurious 'Authentication Failed (unknown:unknown)' Audit Log Entries
bulletins.description: After upgrading to ONTAP 9.17.1, the audit log generates frequent false 'Authentication failed' messages for user 'unknown:unknown', creating noise that can obscure genuine security events.

ontap: Check KB CONTAP-391035 for fix P-release

bulletins.mitigation: Filter audit log noise by user 'unknown:unknown' in SIEM. Cross-reference with actual security events. Apply fix P-release when available.

bulletins.title: ONTAP Manual Upgrade Fails — Signature Validation Error (Post-Sept 2025 Builds)
bulletins.description: Using 'system node image update' (manual upgrade) from ONTAP builds released before September 9, 2025 to builds released after that date fails with: 'Failed to verify the signatures of the image.' Also affects upgrades from ONTAP 9.8 or 9.9.1.

ontap: Use ANDU (automated upgrade) instead — no software fix for manual upgrade path

bulletins.mitigation: Use ANDU via 'cluster image update' CLI or System Manager instead of 'system node image update'. Multi-stage upgrade required from 9.8 or 9.9.1. Contact NetApp Support if ANDU unavailable.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.16.1–9.18.1 NVMe Host Deallocate Does Not Clear All Unused Namespace Blocks
bulletins.description: An issue in ONTAP 9.16.1, 9.17.1, and 9.18.1 means NVMe deallocate commands from a host do not clear all unused blocks from the NVMe namespace, preventing full space reclamation.
bulletins.mitigation: Monitor NVMe namespace utilization. If space reclamation is critical, open a support case referencing the NVMe deallocate issue. Consider periodic space rebalancing.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.16.1 Upgrade May Disable Priority Flow Control (PFC) on CX6 Non-Shared Cluster Ports
bulletins.description: Following an upgrade to ONTAP 9.16.1+, Priority Flow Control (PFC) may be disabled on Mellanox CX6 non-shared cluster ports, potentially impacting cluster interconnect performance in RoCE environments.

ontap: Check KB for specific P-release fix

bulletins.mitigation: Verify PFC configuration post-upgrade via 'network interface show'. Re-enable PFC if disabled. Reference NetApp KB for CX6 PFC restoration procedure.

bulletins.title: ONTAP 9.18.1 Intel NIC Communication Failures with Cisco Nexus 5K/7K
bulletins.description: After upgrading to ONTAP 9.18.1, certain Intel NICs may experience communication failures with Cisco Nexus 5K and 7K switches, causing data path interruptions.

ontap: Check KB for specific P-release fix or NIC firmware workaround

bulletins.mitigation: Before upgrading to 9.18.1, verify Intel NIC and Cisco Nexus switch firmware compatibility. Check NetApp KB for specific affected NIC/switch combinations and workarounds.

bulletins.title: MySQL Server Vulnerabilities in NetApp Management Products (Oracle CPU Recurring)
bulletins.description: Multiple advisories are periodically issued for MySQL vulnerabilities in bundled MySQL components across NetApp management products (SnapCenter, Active IQ Unified Manager). Arise from Oracle Critical Patch Updates.

snapcenter: Latest patches incorporating Oracle CPU updates

bulletins.mitigation: Apply latest NetApp management software patches incorporating Oracle CPU updates on a quarterly basis.

bulletins.title: Microsoft Kerberos KDC Update — CIFS/SMB Authentication Disruption Risk
bulletins.description: A Microsoft security update changing Kerberos/KDC behavior (KB5073381) can disrupt CIFS/SMB authentication on ONTAP clusters when AD accounts have a blank msds-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute, causing authentication failures for CIFS clients.

ontap: Configuration fix — ensure AES-128/AES-256 encryption types set on CIFS SVM AD accounts

bulletins.mitigation: Ensure ONTAP CIFS servers use AES-128/AES-256 encryption types. Update AD account msds-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute. Test in staging before applying Windows updates. Review NetApp KB Kerberos compatibility guidance.

bulletins.title: ONTAP Apache HTTP Server mod_rewrite URL Mapping — Critical
bulletins.description: A vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server mod_rewrite (CVE-2024-38475, CVSS 9.1) allows an attacker to map URLs to filesystem locations that are permitted but not intentionally reachable, leading to source code disclosure or remote code execution in ONTAP and other NetApp products.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to fixed ONTAP patch release (9.12.1P16, 9.13.1P14, 9.14.1P8, 9.15.1P3, or 9.16.1+). Check NTAP-20240712-0001 for your specific branch fix.

bulletins.title: ONTAP Apache HTTP Server Content-Type Legacy Config Source Disclosure
bulletins.description: A vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server (CVE-2024-40725) related to incorrect handling of legacy content-type configurations can lead to source code disclosure of local content (e.g., PHP scripts served instead of interpreted) in ONTAP.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to ONTAP patch incorporating Apache HTTP Server 2.4.62+. Verify fixed patch level against NTAP-20240808-0007.

bulletins.title: ONTAP PHP ldap_escape Buffer Overflow — Critical CVSS 9.8
bulletins.description: A buffer overflow in PHP's ldap_escape() function (CVE-2024-11236) on 32-bit systems causes an integer overflow resulting in an out-of-bounds write, potentially enabling remote code execution or DoS in ONTAP and other NetApp products incorporating PHP.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade to ONTAP patch release incorporating PHP 8.1.30+, 8.2.24+, or 8.3.12+. Check NTAP-20241220-0008 for specific branch patch versions.

bulletins.title: ONTAP PHP Multiple Vulnerabilities (8.1/8.2/8.3 Pre-Fix Versions)
bulletins.description: Multiple PHP vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-8927, CVE-2024-8925, CVE-2024-8926, CVE-2024-9026) in PHP versions prior to 8.1.30/8.2.24/8.3.12 affect ONTAP 9.12.1 and higher, potentially leading to information disclosure, data modification, or DoS.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch incorporating PHP 8.1.30+, 8.2.24+, or 8.3.12+. Check NTAP-20241101-0003 for your specific ONTAP branch fix version.

bulletins.title: Python tarfile Path Traversal — Arbitrary File Write Outside Extraction Directory
bulletins.description: A path traversal flaw in Python's tarfile.extractall() with filter='data' or filter='tar' allows attackers to use malicious symlinks/hardlinks in tar archives to write arbitrary files outside the intended extraction directory, enabling potential RCE via configuration file overwrite.

ontap.to: current — check NetApp advisory for affected Python versions

ontap: ONTAP patches incorporating fixed Python version — check security.netapp.com

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp patches incorporating fixed Python. Avoid processing untrusted tar archives. Isolate any archive extraction processes from privileged file paths.

bulletins.title: Apache HTTP Server CVE-2024-47252 in ONTAP (part of NTAP-20250718-0013)
bulletins.description: CVE-2024-47252 is one of multiple Apache HTTP Server vulnerabilities in NTAP-20250718-0013 affecting various NetApp products. Potential impacts include information disclosure or denial of service.

ontap: ONTAP patches incorporating Apache HTTP Server 2.4.64+

bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch release incorporating Apache HTTP Server 2.4.64+. Covered by NTAP-20250718-0013.

bulletins.category: Authentication Bypass / BMC Firmware — CISA KEV CONFIRMED
bulletins.title: AMI MegaRAC SPx BMC Authentication Bypass — Actively Exploited (CISA KEV)
bulletins.description: Authentication bypass in AMI MegaRAC SPx BMC firmware (CVSS 10.0). Attackers modify X-Server-Addr or Host HTTP headers to impersonate the BMC Redfish host interface, gaining unauthenticated full BMC control. Enables data destruction, persistent malware installation, and system bricking. CONFIRMED IN CISA KEV — actively exploited in the wild. Affects StorageGRID SG6160, SGF6112, SG110, SG1100 appliances.

storagegrid: Firmware update AMI MegaRAC SPx 12.7+ or 13.5+; StorageGRID 11.9.0.7+ hotfix

bulletins.mitigation: URGENT — CISA KEV confirmed active exploitation. Apply firmware update to AMI MegaRAC SPx 12.7+ or 13.5+ immediately. Apply StorageGRID hotfix 11.9.0.7+. Isolate BMC management interfaces from untrusted networks. Unaffected models: SG6060, SGF6024, SG100, SG1000.

bulletins.title: Golang x/crypto SSH Library Authentication Bypass — Trident / NetApp
bulletins.description: Authorization bypass in golang.org/x/crypto SSH library due to misuse of ServerConfig.PublicKeyCallback. Applications making incorrect assumptions about which key authenticated a connection may allow authentication bypass. Affects NetApp Trident and Trident Protect.

trident: Updated Trident releases with patched Go crypto dependency

bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Trident to latest release incorporating fixed golang.org/x/crypto. Verify Trident's Go dependency versions. Check NetApp advisory for specific Trident version fix.

bulletins.category: Stack Buffer Overflow / Remote Code Execution
bulletins.title: OpenSSL Stack Buffer Overflow in CMS AuthEnvelopedData — ONTAP 9.18.1
bulletins.description: Stack buffer overflow in OpenSSL (3.0–3.6) when parsing CMS (Auth)EnvelopedData messages with maliciously crafted AEAD parameters. Could enable remote code execution or DoS. Affects ONTAP 9.18.1 which incorporates vulnerable OpenSSL 3.x. SnapCenter confirmed NOT affected.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP 9.18.1 patch incorporating fixed OpenSSL. If urgency high, consider reverting to 9.17.1P4+ (not affected) until 9.18.1 patch available. Monitor NetApp advisory for fix release date.

bulletins.title: Golang Compiler Integer Overflow — Trident / Trident Protect
bulletins.description: Integer overflow/underflow vulnerability in Golang compiler affecting arithmetic over loop induction variables, potentially causing memory corruption. Affects NetApp Trident and Trident Protect due to bundled Go runtime. Part of NTAP-20260424-0001.

trident: Latest Trident release with patched Go runtime

bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Trident and Trident Protect to latest supported release. Check NTAP-20260424-0001 for specific version fix.

bulletins.title: ONTAP Select Deploy Administration Utility Read-Only Privilege Escalation
bulletins.description: A read-only user of ONTAP Select Deploy administration utility can escalate privileges to modify or delete Deploy-managed data in versions 9.12.1.x, 9.13.1.x, and 9.14.1.x.

ontap: Per NTAP advisory — check mysupport.netapp.com for ONTAP Select Deploy patch

bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP Select Deploy patch. Restrict access to Deploy utility to admin-only accounts. Audit all Deploy users for unauthorized privilege levels.

bulletins.title: ONTAP object-store profiler Command Sensitive Information Disclosure
bulletins.description: When an administrative user executes the 'object-store profiler' command in ONTAP 9.4 and higher, sensitive information can be disclosed to unprivileged attackers who have access to the output.

ontap: Per NetApp advisory — specific patch per branch

bulletins.mitigation: Restrict 'object-store profiler' command execution to trusted admins. Apply NetApp advisory patch. Do not share CLI output containing profiler data with untrusted parties.

bulletins.title: ONTAP OpenSSL TLSv1.3 Unbounded Memory Growth DoS
bulletins.description: A Denial of Service vulnerability in OpenSSL TLSv1.3 causes unbounded memory growth in non-default configurations using SSL_OP_NO_TICKET. Affects multiple NetApp products including ONTAP.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch release from NTAP-20240503-0013. Review TLS configuration — SSL_OP_NO_TICKET is non-default. Limit management interface TLS connections from untrusted sources.

bulletins.title: Linux Kernel Vulnerability — StorageGRID Hotfix Required
bulletins.description: A Linux kernel vulnerability affecting StorageGRID and other NetApp products. Successful exploitation could lead to disclosure of sensitive information or Denial of Service. A StorageGRID hotfix 11.9.0.7 is required; reboot of affected nodes required post-patch.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply StorageGRID hotfix 11.9.0.7 immediately. Schedule node reboots for affected appliances. Check NTAP-20250613-0011 for full product list.

bulletins.title: libxslt Use-After-Free in xsltGetInheritedNsList — ONTAP / Multiple Products
bulletins.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in libxslt xsltGetInheritedNsList function related to exclusion of result prefixes. Can lead to information disclosure, data modification, or Denial of Service in NetApp products incorporating libxslt prior to 1.1.43.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch incorporating libxslt 1.1.43+. Related to CVE-2025-24855 in same advisory group. Check NetApp security portal for per-version fix.

bulletins.title: libcurl HTTP/2 Server Push Memory Leak — ONTAP / Multiple Products
bulletins.description: HTTP/2 server push memory leak in libcurl 7.44.0–8.6.0. When excessive headers are received with server push enabled, libcurl fails to free all allocated headers on abort, causing DoS via memory exhaustion in NetApp products incorporating curl.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch incorporating curl 8.7.1+. If HTTP/2 server push is used, monitor for memory growth. Check NetApp security portal for per-version fix.

bulletins.title: Node.js Denial of Service in Multiple NetApp Products
bulletins.description: Node.js DoS vulnerability affecting multiple NetApp products that bundle Node.js (≤18.19.0, 19.x–20.11.0, 21.x–21.6.1) including Active IQ Unified Manager, ONTAP tools for VMware vSphere, and others. Remote unauthenticated attacker can cause service unavailability.

ontap: Per NTAP-20240517-0008 — update Node.js bundled components

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp product patches incorporating Node.js 18.20.0+, 20.12.0+, or 21.7.0+. Check NTAP-20240517-0008 for per-product fix versions.

bulletins.title: Golang x/net/html DOM Construction XSS — Trident / NetApp Products
bulletins.description: XSS/DOM manipulation vulnerability in golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer (prior to 0.38.0). HTML tokenizer incorrectly interprets unquoted attribute values ending in a solidus (/) as self-closing tags, causing incorrect DOM construction and potential XSS in foreign content (math/svg contexts). Affects NetApp Trident and other Golang-based products.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Trident to latest release incorporating golang.org/x/net/html 0.38.0+. Check NTAP-20250516-0007 for product-specific fix details.

bulletins.title: FreeBSD UMTX Use-After-Free — ONTAP 9 Kernel Privilege Escalation (CVSS 10.0)
bulletins.description: Use-After-Free in FreeBSD kernel UMTX subsystem (UMTX_SHM_DESTROY). Race condition on concurrent anonymous shared memory unmapping leads to kernel panic, arbitrary code execution at kernel privilege, or Capsicum sandbox bypass. All ONTAP 9 versions incorporating FreeBSD ≤14.1p3 are potentially affected.

ontap: ONTAP patch incorporating FreeBSD 14.1-p4+ or 13.3-p6+ — check NTAP-20240916-0001

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NTAP-20240916-0001 patch immediately. This is a critical kernel-level UAF requiring OS-level patch. Restrict direct kernel access. Check advisory for ONTAP branch-specific fix versions.

bulletins.title: SQLite Integer Overflow in SnapCenter Server — CVSS 9.8
bulletins.description: SQLite memory corruption vulnerability (CVE-2025-6965, CVSS 9.8): aggregate term count can exceed available columns causing integer overflow/truncation and memory corruption. When processing malicious SQL, could lead to DoS or arbitrary code execution. Affects SnapCenter Server and Active IQ Unified Manager which bundle SQLite.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade SnapCenter to latest release incorporating SQLite 3.50.2+. Restrict SnapCenter server access to authorized administrators only. Check NTAP-20250718-0001 for specific fix versions.

bulletins.title: Golang Standard Library Memory Corruption — Trident / ONTAP Tools
bulletins.description: Golang standard library vulnerability allowing authenticated network-based attackers to achieve code execution or cause significant data compromise in applications built with affected Go versions (< 1.25.9, < 1.26.2). Affects NetApp Trident and ONTAP tools for VMware vSphere. Part of NTAP-20260424-0001.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Trident and ONTAP tools for VMware to latest release incorporating Go 1.25.9+ or 1.26.2+. Check NTAP-20260424-0001 for specific product fix versions.

bulletins.title: Apache HTTP Server mod_proxy Encoding Bypass — ONTAP 9
bulletins.description: Encoding problem in Apache HTTP Server mod_proxy (CVE-2024-38473) allows specially crafted request URLs to bypass authentication controls and reach backend services inappropriately. Part of the same advisory batch as CVE-2024-38475. Affects ONTAP 9 systems with management interfaces exposed via reverse proxy.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply same NTAP-20240712-0001 patch as CVE-2024-38475 (Apache HTTP Server 2.4.60+). Restrict management interface exposure through reverse proxies. Do not expose ONTAP management to internet.

bulletins.title: ISC BIND DNS Flood DoS — ONTAP 9 DNS Load Balancing
bulletins.description: ISC BIND vulnerability (CVE-2024-0760): remote attacker can flood DNS messages over TCP causing DNS service instability (DoS). Affects ONTAP 9 v9.14.1+ systems configured for DNS load balancing.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP patch from NTAP-20240731-0004. Rate-limit TCP DNS connections at network layer. Review DNS load balancing configuration. Consider restricting DNS listener to management networks only.

bulletins.title: 7-Zip MoTW Bypass in Active IQ Unified Manager — Arbitrary Code Execution Risk
bulletins.description: Mark-of-the-Web (MoTW) Bypass in 7-Zip < 24.09 (CVE-2025-0411): When extracting archives from untrusted sources, 7-Zip fails to propagate the Windows security marker to extracted files, allowing bypass of Windows security warnings and potential code execution. Affects Active IQ Unified Manager 9.16x bundling 7-Zip 23.x/24.x < 24.09.
bulletins.mitigation: Update 7-Zip to 24.09+ within Active IQ Unified Manager deployment. Do not extract archives from untrusted sources on AIQUM hosts. Apply OS-level controls restricting archive extraction.

bulletins.title: StorageGRID SSH Cryptographic Flaw — Man-in-the-Middle Information Disclosure
bulletins.description: SSH cryptographic implementation flaw in StorageGRID (CVE-2024-21988) enables information disclosure via complex Man-in-the-Middle attacks. Affects StorageGRID < 11.7.0.9 and < 11.8.0.5.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade StorageGRID to 11.7.0.9+ or 11.8.0.5+. Enforce network segmentation on StorageGRID admin networks to reduce MitM attack surface. Use only approved SSH key types.

bulletins.title: ONTAP Select Deploy Hard-Coded Default Credentials
bulletins.description: Hard-coded (default) credentials in ONTAP Select Deploy administration utility (CVE-2024-21990) could allow unauthorized access to configuration data or credential modification by any party with network access to the Deploy utility.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade ONTAP Select Deploy to 9.15.1+. Change all default credentials immediately. Restrict network access to Deploy utility to trusted admin networks only. Audit all Deploy accounts.

bulletins.title: TPM 2.0 Library Out-of-Bounds Read — ONTAP 9.18.1+ Platforms
bulletins.description: Out-of-bounds read in TPM 2.0 reference library CryptHmacSign function (CVE-2025-2884) due to insufficient signature scheme validation. Requires physical access and ability to install custom packages bypassing ONTAP protections. Can lead to sensitive info disclosure or TPM denial of service. Affects ONTAP on newer platforms with TPM 2.0.
bulletins.mitigation: Apply ONTAP 9.18.1 patch incorporating fixed TPM 2.0 library. Enforce physical access controls on all ONTAP nodes — this requires physical or elevated local access to exploit. Do not allow untrusted custom package installations.

bulletins.title: OpenSSL Certificate Name Checking DoS — ONTAP Select Deploy
bulletins.description: OpenSSL Denial of Service (CVE-2024-6119): application may crash during certificate name checking in certain configurations. Affects ONTAP Select Deploy administration utility incorporating vulnerable OpenSSL versions.

ontap: OpenSSL 3.3.2+; apply ONTAP Select Deploy patch

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NTAP-20240903-0001 patch for ONTAP Select Deploy. Upgrade OpenSSL to 3.3.2+. Restrict certificate validation sources to trusted CAs only.

bulletins.title: PHP Additional CVEs (Out-of-bounds Read / DoS) — ONTAP PHP Component
bulletins.description: Additional PHP vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-11233 — out-of-bounds read leading to DoS/info disclosure; CVE-2024-11234 — resource exhaustion DoS via specially crafted input) in PHP < 8.3.14. Affect ONTAP 9 PHP components under the same NTAP-20241220-0008 advisory as CVE-2024-11236.

ontap: ONTAP patch incorporating PHP 8.3.14+ — check NTAP-20241220-0008

bulletins.mitigation: Apply same ONTAP patch as CVE-2024-11236 (NTAP-20241220-0008), which also covers CVE-2024-11233 and CVE-2024-11234.

bulletins.title: OpenSSH ProxyCommand Injection via Control Characters — Multiple NetApp Products
bulletins.description: OpenSSH client vulnerabilities: CVE-2025-61984 allows control characters in usernames via ProxyCommand %n expansion to cause code execution; CVE-2025-61985 allows null characters in ssh:// URIs with ProxyCommand to achieve code execution. ONTAP 9 confirmed NOT susceptible. Affects other NetApp products using OpenSSH client < 10.1.

ontap.to: ONTAP 9 NOT affected — check other products in NTAP-20251017-0010

ontap: OpenSSH 10.1+; apply advisory patch for affected products

bulletins.mitigation: ONTAP 9 itself is NOT affected. For other NetApp products (AIQUM, ONTAP tools) using OpenSSH client, upgrade to OpenSSH 10.1+. Apply NTAP-20251017-0010 patch for affected product list.

bulletins.title: Intel Ethernet Controller Info Disclosure — Specific ONTAP Platforms
bulletins.description: Intel Ethernet Controller vulnerability (CVE-2025-24296) allowing sensitive information disclosure in privileged local context. Affects ONTAP 9 on specific newer platforms using Intel E810 / Intel Ethernet Controller: A50, A30, A20, C60, C30, FAS50, A1K, A90, A70, C80, FAS90, FAS70.

ontap: ONTAP 9.18.1+ or platform-specific firmware patch — check NTAP-20250513-0002

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NTAP-20250513-0002 firmware patch. Restrict local access to affected ONTAP controller nodes. Upgrade to ONTAP 9.18.1+ or apply Intel NIC firmware update per advisory.

bulletins.title: 7-Zip Archive Processing Vulnerability — Multiple NetApp Products
bulletins.description: 7-Zip vulnerability (CVE-2025-11002) leading to potential data disclosure, modification, or DoS when processing specially crafted archive files. Affects multiple NetApp products bundling 7-Zip < 25.x.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade 7-Zip to 25.x in all affected NetApp product deployments. Do not process untrusted archive files on NetApp product servers. Check NTAP-20260213-0011 for per-product fix versions.

bulletins.title: Astra Trident Driver PATH Hijacking — Local Privilege Escalation
bulletins.description: PATH hijacking vulnerability in Astra Trident driver (CVE-2026-24051, CVSS 7.8). Local privilege escalation via environment variable manipulation allowing execution of arbitrary binaries with elevated privileges. Affects Astra Trident v24.02 and earlier.
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Astra Trident to v26.02 or later immediately. Restrict local access to Kubernetes worker nodes running Trident. Validate PATH environment variable integrity in Trident deployment.

bulletins.title: Active IQ Config Advisor 6.7.3 — Hard-Coded Credentials (CVE-2026-22054)
bulletins.description: Hard-coded credentials in Active IQ Config Advisor 6.7.3 allow an authenticated low-privilege attacker to perform unauthorized AutoSupport operations. NetApp has noted public discussion of this vulnerability exists. Affects the standalone diagnostic tool, not ONTAP cluster software. CVSS v4.0 5.3 (AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:N/SC:L/SI:N/SA:N).
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Active IQ Config Advisor to version 6.7.4 or later. Limit tool access to authorized administrators only. No ONTAP cluster-level patch required.

bulletins.title: Active IQ OneCollect 2.7.3 — Hard-Coded Credentials (CVE-2026-22055)
bulletins.description: Companion advisory to NTAP-20260603-0001. Hard-coded credentials in Active IQ OneCollect 2.7.3 allow an authenticated low-privilege attacker to perform unauthorized AutoSupport operations via the same credential class of vulnerability. NetApp is aware of public discussion; no known in-the-wild exploitation reported as of 2026-07-13. Both advisories (0001/0002) were bundled together under CERT-FR CERTFR-2026-AVI-0686. Affects the standalone data-collection tool, not ONTAP cluster software. CVSS v4.0 5.3 (AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:N/SC:L/SI:N/SA:N).
bulletins.mitigation: Upgrade Active IQ OneCollect to version 2.7.4 or later. Restrict tool access to authorized administrators. No ONTAP cluster-level patch required. Cross-reference NTAP-20260603-0001 (Config Advisor companion advisory).

bulletins.category: Remote Code Execution / Third-Party Component (Ingress NGINX)
bulletins.title: Ingress NGINX Controller Vulnerabilities in NetApp Products (IngressNightmare)
bulletins.description: Multiple critical and high-severity vulnerabilities in the Ingress NGINX controller (collectively known as 'IngressNightmare') affect NetApp products that bundle Ingress NGINX as a component, primarily BlueXP and Kubernetes-adjacent NetApp integrations. CVE-2025-1974 (CVSS 9.8) allows unauthenticated remote code execution via the Kubernetes admission controller webhook when accessible from within the pod network. Not core ONTAP cluster software. Check applicability against your specific product/version using the NetApp advisory.

generic: Update Ingress NGINX to patched version per advisory

bulletins.mitigation: 1. Identify if any NetApp product in your environment bundles Ingress NGINX (check advisory for product list). 2. Upgrade affected Ingress NGINX components to patched versions. 3. As interim mitigation: restrict network access to the Kubernetes admission controller webhook endpoint (TCP 8443) to only the Kubernetes API server, preventing external pod-network access.

bulletins.title: OpenSSH sftp Client Path Traversal — NetApp Products (OpenSSH < 10.4)
bulletins.description: An attacker-controlled sftp server can write files to arbitrary locations on the client filesystem via path traversal in returned filenames, potentially enabling remote code execution on the sftp client host. Affects NetApp products bundling OpenSSH prior to version 10.4. NetApp advisory published 2026-07-17, updated 2026-07-21. No known in-the-wild exploitation reported.

generic.note: Affects all NetApp products bundling OpenSSH < 10.4 — check NetApp Security Portal per product for specific versions

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp product-specific patch bundles that incorporate OpenSSH 10.4p1+. Check the NetApp Security Advisory portal for the complete list of affected products and per-version patch availability. As an interim measure, restrict sftp client connections to only trusted, known-good servers.

bulletins.category: HTTP Header Injection / XSS / Session Hijacking
bulletins.title: Apache ActiveMQ HTTP Header Injection via MessageServlet — NetApp Products
bulletins.description: HTTP header injection via JMS message properties in Apache ActiveMQ's MessageServlet allows attackers to inject or overwrite HTTP security headers, enabling cross-site scripting (XSS), session hijacking, and clickjacking attacks. Affects NetApp products bundling Apache ActiveMQ prior to 5.19.7 or 6.0.0–6.2.5. NetApp advisory updated 2026-07-21. Note: NetApp CVSS of 8.8 is higher than NVD base score of 6.1. No known in-the-wild exploitation reported.

generic.note: Affects all NetApp products bundling ActiveMQ < 5.19.7 or 6.0.0–6.2.5 — check NetApp Security Portal per product for specific versions

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp product-specific patches that upgrade the bundled ActiveMQ to 5.19.7+ or 6.2.6+. The MessageServlet is deprecated and disabled by default in patched versions. Check the NetApp Security Advisory portal for the affected product list and per-version patch availability.

bulletins.title: Spring Boot RabbitMQ SSL Bundle Hostname Verification Bypass — NetApp Products
bulletins.description: Spring Boot RabbitMQ auto-configuration omits hostname verification when an SSL bundle is used, allowing a network-adjacent attacker to intercept or alter broker communications via a man-in-the-middle attack. Affects NetApp products using Spring Boot 4.0.0–4.0.5 or 3.5.0–3.5.13. NetApp advisory updated 2026-07-21. No known in-the-wild exploitation reported.

generic.note: Affects all NetApp products bundling Spring Boot 4.0.0–4.0.5 or 3.5.0–3.5.13 — check NetApp Security Portal per product for specific versions

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp product-specific patches that upgrade Spring Boot to 4.0.6+ or 3.5.14+. Check the NetApp Security Advisory portal for affected product list and patch availability per product version.

bulletins.category: Use-After-Free / Linux Kernel KVM / Guest-to-Host Escape
bulletins.title: Linux Kernel KVM Shadow Paging Use-After-Free "Januscape" — NetApp Products
bulletins.description: A use-after-free flaw in the Linux kernel's KVM x86 shadow MMU: when a host PDE mapping is modified from within a guest, an unexpected role mismatch can allow a guest-to-host escape, arbitrary code execution with root privileges on the host, or host panic (denial of service). In environments where /dev/kvm is world-accessible, an unprivileged local user may also trigger the vulnerability. Affects NetApp products running Linux-based host operating systems (primarily StorageGRID appliances and Linux-based management components). NetApp advisory published 2026-07-10. No known in-the-wild exploitation reported.

storagegrid.to: current — check NetApp Security Portal for affected appliance models

storagegrid: StorageGRID patches incorporating fixed Linux kernel (released early July 2026) — check NetApp Security Portal

bulletins.mitigation: Apply patched Linux kernel from your distribution (patched kernels released early July 2026). Workaround: disable nested virtualization (/sys/module/kvm_intel/parameters/nested or kvm_amd equivalent). Check the NetApp Security Portal for StorageGRID appliance-specific patch bundles.

bulletins.category: Use-After-Free / Linux Kernel rtmutex / Local Privilege Escalation
bulletins.title: Linux Kernel rtmutex Priority-Inheritance Use-After-Free "GhostLock" — NetApp Products
bulletins.description: A use-after-free in the Linux kernel's real-time mutex (rtmutex) priority-inheritance path within the futex subsystem allows a local unprivileged attacker to escalate privileges to root or escape container isolation. Affects NetApp products running Linux-based host operating systems (primarily StorageGRID appliances and Linux-based management components). NetApp advisory published 2026-07-17. No known in-the-wild exploitation reported.

storagegrid.to: current — check NetApp Security Portal for affected appliance models

storagegrid: StorageGRID patches incorporating fixed Linux kernel — check NetApp Security Portal

bulletins.mitigation: Apply patched Linux kernel from vendor distribution. Check the NetApp Security Portal for StorageGRID appliance-specific patch bundles and affected version ranges for your specific appliance hardware model.

bulletins.title: FreeBSD libnv Heap-Based Buffer Overflow in NetApp ONTAP
bulletins.summary: A heap-based buffer overflow in the FreeBSD libnv library (used in ONTAP) allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to cause a kernel panic (denial of service) or achieve unauthorized data modification. The library fails to validate message size when processing an incoming message header, allowing writes beyond heap allocation bounds. NetApp advisory NTAP-20260501-0002 — Interim status, product version matrix being updated.

ontap.to: current - check NTAP-20260501-0002 for specific affected version ranges

ontap: Patches incorporating fixed FreeBSD libnv — check NetApp Security Advisory NTAP-20260501-0002 for product/version matrix

bulletins.mitigation: Apply NetApp product-specific patches incorporating the fixed FreeBSD libnv library. Monitor the NetApp Security Advisory NTAP-20260501-0002 at https://security.netapp.com/advisory/ntap-20260501-0002/ for the current affected/fixed version matrix as this advisory is Interim and being updated. No known in-the-wild exploitation reported.
